behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily a module handoff/orchestration check: collectors + aggregator + summary-invocation wiring. The behavior text does not require real external contract validation or inbox/file round-trip, and it does not depend on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report job with three source adapters stubbed to return distinct sentinel payloads; assert the exact payload passed into the summary-generation interface contains all three source sections/values.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is cross-module error propagation and fallback rendering. A simulated source failure in-process can reveal whether the pipeline continues and injects the fallback note. Behavior text does not explicitly require real network error-contract checks.
behaviors.test_description: Stub one source client to raise an unavailability exception while the other two succeed; execute the pipeline and assert report generation completes and the affected section contains `data unavailable` while other sections remain populated.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on the quality/factuality of generated narrative content, which is a real model judgment problem. Stubs can verify plumbing but cannot validate whether the model actually preserves key values.
behaviors.test_description: Use real summary-generation model calls on representative fixtures with known key metrics/issues/deploy facts; evaluate that produced narrative includes and does not contradict those key values (with rubric or automated factual checks plus spot human review).

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires an externally visible side effect proven by round-trip readback (`reaches ... inbox`). That is a System-level check under the round-trip effect rule.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled job end-to-end against real reachable dependencies (or production-like endpoints), send to a test mailbox, and verify via mailbox readback that the email arrived with expected subject/body/report content markers.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans real delivery-failure handling plus durable side effects across runs (`saved to local file` and `retried on next scheduled run`). This is best validated with real process/runtime boundaries rather than only in-process mocks.
behaviors.test_description: Force real email delivery failure (e.g., unreachable SMTP/API endpoint in test env), run the job, verify fallback file is written, then trigger the next scheduled run and confirm retry occurs and eventually delivers once service is restored.
